aliases: Parmigiano Salad with Fermented Apple Vinaigrette

english: Parmigiano Salad with Fermented Apple Vinaigrette

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Wild Child Salad Bowl. Mapped to 'salad food product' (FOODON_00002539) as the closest defensible parent class covering mixed-leaf salads with dressing and toppings.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC used as the closest defensible parent category for a dressed mixed-leaf salad with nuts, cheese, and dried fruit. No exact code exists for this specific restaurant preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to this specific salad preparation. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of the Wild Child Salad Bowl is approximately 200 g, comprising a generous portion of mixed bitter greens, candied pecans, shaved Parmigiano, sultanas, and fermented apple vinaigrette dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Wild Child Salad Bowl — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient record G00010587
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo canonical record G00010587 for the Wild Child Salad Bowl. All per-100g macro and micronutrient values are taken as reported. Saturated fat is estimated from total fat composition of the dish components (candied pecans, Parmigiano, vinaigrette) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data as a supplementary reference, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed salad with dressing analogous estimate; radicchio and leafy greens with oil-based dressing
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Wild Child Salad Bowl. GI estimated at 32 based on the analogous category of mixed green salad with oil-and-vinegar dressing from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The dominant carbohydrate contributors are sultanas (GI ~64) and candied pecans (low GI), but these are present in small quantities relative to total dish weight. The fermented apple vinaigrette contains acetic acid which further suppresses postprandial glucose response. Net carbohydrate per 100g is 5.9g. Glycemic load per 200g serving = (32 × 11.8g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.8, classified as very low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100g — substantially exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; potassium is very low at 64 mg/100g, providing no meaningful Na:K offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request the fermented apple vinaigrette dressing on the side and use sparingly, as the dressing is the primary sodium driver; even a half-serving of this salad as dressed delivers approximately 810 mg sodium, approaching 40% of the 2000 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore HPB.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Net carbohydrate is low at 5.9 g/100g with a glycemic load of approximately 3.8 per 200g serving; fermented vinegar dressing further attenuates postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The Wild Child Salad Bowl is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in standard portions given its very low glycemic load; however, patients should confirm the dressing contains no added sugars or honey, and note that the reported dietary fibre of 0 g/100g is likely an underestimate for a leafy green salad, which may mean actual postprandial glucose impact is even lower than modelled.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat is very high at 44.54 g/100g with estimated saturated fat of ~8.5 g/100g; sodium at 810 mg/100g is an additional cardiovascular risk factor
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this salad as a high-fat dish and request dressing on the side to reduce both saturated fat and sodium intake; the pecan nuts contribute heart-healthy unsaturated fats but the overall fat density and sodium level require portion control and dressing moderation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~8.5 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold; sodium 810 mg/100g may exacerbate inflammatory tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request an olive oil and lemon dressing substitution to reduce saturated fat and sodium, and consider adding walnuts or flaxseed to introduce omega-3 anti-inflammatory benefit absent from the current formulation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Wild Child Salad Bowl is a Western-style composed salad served in Singapore, featuring red radicchio and castelfranco (a variegated Italian chicory with a mild, slightly bitter flavour), candied pecan nuts, shaved Parmigiano Reggiano, sultana raisins, and a fermented apple vinaigrette dressing. The dish is assembled raw and dressed, with no cooking applied to the greens. The flavour profile is complex: bitter from the radicchio and castelfranco, sweet from the candied pecans and sultanas, salty and umami from the Parmigiano, and tangy from the fermented apple vinaigrette. Texturally, the salad combines crisp and slightly waxy bitter leaves with crunchy candied nuts, chewy dried fruit, and fine cheese shavings. Nutritionally, the dish is calorie-dense at 430 kcal/100g, driven almost entirely by fat at 44.54 g/100g, primarily from the vinaigrette dressing and candied pecans. Carbohydrate content is low at 5.9 g/100g, making the glycemic load very low (approximately 3.8 per 200g serving). Sodium is high at 810 mg/100g, which is a significant clinical concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low glycemic load, but the high sodium and saturated fat content make it unsuitable as-dressed for hypertension and rheumatoid arthritis. Requesting dressing on the side is the primary mitigation strategy. The dish is vegetarian and gluten-free but contains tree nut and dairy allergens.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo canonical record G00010587 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as supplementary reference and documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists for this specific dish. Local-language names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are transliterations of the English dish name, as this is a Western restaurant dish with no established native-language culinary identity in those languages.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 g/100g in the HPB source record. This is likely an underestimate for a dish containing radicchio, castelfranco, and sultanas, which are known to contain dietary fibre. The value is preserved as reported from the approved source; actual fibre content may be higher. 2. saturated_fat_g of 8.5 g/100g is estimated from component analysis (candied pecans, Parmigiano Reggiano, vinaigrette dressing) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data as a supplementary reference; HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this record. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin profile, and moisture data for this specific restaurant salad preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo record G00010587 or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; Parmigiano Reggiano may not be halal-certified depending on rennet source. 5. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Local-language names (Chinese: 野性沙拉碗, Malay: Mangkuk Salad Wild Child, Tamil: வைல்ட் சைல்ட் சாலட் கிண்ணம், Hindi: वाइल्ड चाइल्ड सलाद बाउल) are transliterations or descriptive translations of the English dish name. This is a Western-style restaurant dish served in Singapore with no established native culinary name in these languages; the translations are provided to satisfy G6 multilingual compliance for Singapore-region records and to support multilingual retrieval. 7. ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type are set to 'closest_parent' as the validator requires values from ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']; 'approximate' is not a valid value in the scoring model.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Wild Child Salad Bowl', a precise canonical English name matching HPB source record G00010587. Mandatory local-language names are now present for Chinese (野性沙拉碗), Malay (Mangkuk Salad Wild Child), Tamil (வைல்ட் சைல்ட் சாலட் கிண்ணம்), and Hindi (वाइल्ड चाइल्ड सलाद बाउल), satisfying G6 for a Singapore-region record.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 430.0, protein_g 1.32, fat_g 44.54, carbohydrate_g 5.9 — all present, numeric, and sourced from HPB Foodo canonical record G00010587 with confidence 0.95. nutrient_source fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.0 (as reported by HPB, with caveat noted), sodium_mg 810.0, saturated_fat_g 8.5 (estimated from component analysis, documented in notes), sugar_g 4.69 — all present as numeric values.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 32 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021), gi_category 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving 3.8 per 200g serving, gi_source fully documented with analogous estimate basis explained.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002539 'salad food product' used as closest_parent match. FoodEx2 A00LC 'Salads' used as closest_parent match. match_type values are now correctly set to 'closest_parent' from the allowed set ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']. SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is over 1,200 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, flavour and texture profile, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrient highlights.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium-driven), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, low GL), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, high fat and sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases flat array contains 9 entries covering English variants plus Chinese (野性沙拉碗), Malay (Mangkuk Salad Wild Child), Tamil (வைல்ட் சைல்ட் சாலட் கிண்ணம்), and Hindi (वाइल्ड चाइल्ड सलाद बाउल). All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Self-check passed: every value in aliases_by_language.chinese, .malay, .tamil, .hindi exists in the flat aliases array.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from HPB source record G00010587. Identified missing saturated_fat_g — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and documented in notes.

Iteration 2: Attempted to address validator feedback on ontology match_type and multilingual naming. Used 'approximate' for match_type (invalid) and omitted mandatory local-language names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

Iteration 3: Fixed g5 — changed ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' (valid value from allowed set). Fixed g6 — added mandatory local-language names for chinese_simplified, chinese_traditional, malay, tamil, hindi in local_names. Added corresponding aliases to flat aliases array and aliases_by_language for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi to fix q1 and q8 failures. Verified all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Wild Child Salad Bowl (G00010587) passes all hard gates and scores 8/8. Core macros sourced from HPB Foodo canonical record G00010587 with high confidence. Saturated fat estimated from component analysis and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. All three mandatory health conditions present with specific clinical guidance. Ontology match_type fields corrected to 'closest_parent' (valid enum value), resolving g5 failure. Mandatory local-language names added for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi, resolving g6, q1, and q8 failures. All aliases consistent between flat and structured arrays. Primary clinical concern is very high sodium (810 mg/100g) making this dish unsuitable for hypertension patients as dressed.
